verses.latin_vulgate: Audite verbum quod locutus est Dominus super vos, domus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֣וּ אֶת־הַדָּבָ֗ר אֲשֶׁ֨ר דִּבֶּ֧ר יְהֹוָ֛ה עֲלֵיכֶ֖ם בֵּ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Hear ye the word which the Lord speaketh unto you, O house of Israel:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Juxta vias gentium nolite discere,  et a signis cæli nolite metuere, quæ timent gentes,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה אֶל־דֶּ֤רֶךְ הַגּוֹיִם֙ אַל־תִּלְמָ֔דוּ וּמֵאֹת֥וֹת הַשָּׁמַ֖יִם אַל־תֵּחָ֑תּוּ כִּי־יֵחַ֥תּוּ הַגּוֹיִ֖ם מֵהֵֽמָּה׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, Learn not the way of the heathen, and be not dismayed at the signs of heaven; for the heathen are dismayed at them.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia leges populorum vanæ sunt.  Quia lignum de saltu præcidit opus manus artificis in ascia :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־חֻקּ֥וֹת הָעַמִּ֖ים הֶ֣בֶל ה֑וּא כִּי־עֵץ֙ מִיַּ֣עַר כְּרָת֔וֹ מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה יְדֵי־חָרָ֖שׁ בַּֽמַּעֲצָֽד׃
verses.king_james: For the customs of the people are vain: for one cutteth a tree out of the forest, the work of the hands of the workman, with the axe.

verses.latin_vulgate: argento et auro decoravit illud :  clavis et malleis compegit, ut non dissolvatur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּכֶ֥סֶף וּבְזָהָ֖ב יְיַפֵּ֑הוּ בְּמַסְמְר֧וֹת וּבְמַקָּב֛וֹת יְחַזְּק֖וּם וְל֥וֹא יָפִֽיק׃
verses.king_james: They deck it with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers, that it move not.

verses.latin_vulgate: in similitudinem palmæ fabricata sunt,  et non loquentur :  portata tollentur,  quia incedere non valent.  Nolite ergo timere ea,  quia nec male possunt facere, nec bene.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּתֹ֨מֶר מִקְשָׁ֥ה הֵ֙מָּה֙ וְלֹ֣א יְדַבֵּ֔רוּ נָשׂ֥וֹא יִנָּשׂ֖וּא כִּ֣י לֹ֣א יִצְעָ֑דוּ אַל־תִּֽירְא֤וּ מֵהֶם֙ כִּי־לֹ֣א יָרֵ֔עוּ וְגַם־הֵיטֵ֖יב אֵ֥ין אוֹתָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: They are upright as the palm tree, but speak not: they must needs be borne, because they cannot go. Be not afraid of them; for they cannot do evil, neither also is it in them to do good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est similis tui, Domine :  magnus es tu,  et magnum nomen tuum in fortitudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵאֵ֥ין כָּמ֖וֹךָ יְהֹוָ֑ה גָּד֥וֹל אַתָּ֛ה וְגָד֥וֹל שִׁמְךָ֖ בִּגְבוּרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Forasmuch as there is none like unto thee, O Lord; thou art great, and thy name is great in might.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis non timebit te, o Rex gentium ?  tuum est enim decus :  inter cunctos sapientes gentium,  et in universis regnis eorum,  nullus est similis tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֣י לֹ֤א יִרָֽאֲךָ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ הַגּוֹיִ֔ם כִּ֥י לְךָ֖ יָאָ֑תָה כִּ֣י בְכׇל־חַכְמֵ֧י הַגּוֹיִ֛ם וּבְכׇל־מַלְכוּתָ֖ם מֵאֵ֥ין כָּמֽוֹךָ׃
verses.king_james: Who would not fear thee, O King of nations? for to thee doth it appertain: forasmuch as among all the wise men of the nations, and in all their kingdoms, there is none like unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pariter insipientes et fatui probabuntur :  doctrina vanitatis eorum lignum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְאַחַ֖ת יִבְעֲר֣וּ וְיִכְסָ֑לוּ מוּסַ֥ר הֲבָלִ֖ים עֵ֥ץ הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: But they are altogether brutish and foolish: the stock is a doctrine of vanities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Argentum involutum de Tharsis affertur,  et aurum de Ophaz :  opus artificis et manus ærarii.  Hyacinthus et purpura indumentum eorum :  opus artificum universa hæc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֶּ֣סֶף מְרֻקָּ֞ע מִתַּרְשִׁ֣ישׁ יוּבָ֗א וְזָהָב֙ מֵֽאוּפָ֔ז מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה חָרָ֖שׁ וִידֵ֣י צוֹרֵ֑ף תְּכֵ֤לֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן֙ לְבוּשָׁ֔ם מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה חֲכָמִ֖ים כֻּלָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Silver spread into plates is brought from Tarshish, and gold from Uphaz, the work of the workman, and of the hands of the founder: blue and purple is their clothing: they are all the work of cunning men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus autem Deus verus est,  ipse Deus vivens,  et rex sempiternus.  Ab indignatione ejus commovebitur terra,  et non sustinebunt gentes comminationem ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהִים֙ אֱמֶ֔ת הֽוּא־אֱלֹהִ֥ים חַיִּ֖ים וּמֶ֣לֶךְ עוֹלָ֑ם מִקִּצְפּוֹ֙ תִּרְעַ֣שׁ הָאָ֔רֶץ וְלֹא־יָכִ֥לוּ גוֹיִ֖ם זַעְמֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But the Lord is the true God, he is the living God, and an everlasting king: at his wrath the earth shall tremble, and the nations shall not be able to abide his indignation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic ergo dicetis eis :  Dii qui cælos et terram non fecerunt,  pereant de terra et de his quæ sub cælo sunt !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּדְנָה֙ תֵּאמְר֣וּן לְה֔וֹם אֱלָ֣הַיָּ֔א דִּֽי־שְׁמַיָּ֥א וְאַרְקָ֖א לָ֣א עֲבַ֑דוּ יֵאבַ֧דוּ מֵאַרְעָ֛א וּמִן־תְּח֥וֹת שְׁמַיָּ֖א אֵֽלֶּה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thus shall ye say unto them, The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth, even they shall perish from the earth, and from under these heavens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui facit terram in fortitudine sua,  præparat orbem in sapientia sua,  et prudentia sua extendit cælos :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹשֵׂ֥ה אֶ֙רֶץ֙ בְּכֹח֔וֹ מֵכִ֥ין תֵּבֵ֖ל בְּחׇכְמָת֑וֹ וּבִתְבוּנָת֖וֹ נָטָ֥ה שָׁמָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his discretion.

verses.latin_vulgate: ad vocem suam dat multitudinem aquarum in cælo,  et elevat nebulas ab extremitatibus terræ :  fulgura in pluviam facit,  et educit ventum de thesauris suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְק֨וֹל תִּתּ֜וֹ הֲמ֥וֹן מַ֙יִם֙ בַּשָּׁמַ֔יִם וַיַּעֲלֶ֥ה נְשִׂאִ֖ים מִקְצֵ֣ה (ארץ) [הָאָ֑רֶץ] בְּרָקִ֤ים לַמָּטָר֙ עָשָׂ֔ה וַיּ֥וֹצֵא ר֖וּחַ מֵאֹצְרֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus factus est omnis homo a scientia :  confusus est artifex omnis in sculptili,  quoniam falsum est quod conflavit,  et non est spiritus in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִבְעַ֤ר כׇּל־אָדָם֙ מִדַּ֔עַת הֹבִ֥ישׁ כׇּל־צוֹרֵ֖ף מִפָּ֑סֶל כִּ֛י שֶׁ֥קֶר נִסְכּ֖וֹ וְלֹא־ר֥וּחַ בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Every man is brutish in his knowledge: every founder is confounded by the graven image: for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vana sunt, et opus risu dignum :  in tempore visitationis suæ peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶ֣בֶל הֵ֔מָּה מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה תַּעְתֻּעִ֑ים בְּעֵ֥ת פְּקֻדָּתָ֖ם יֹאבֵֽדוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are vanity, and the work of errors: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est his similis pars Jacob :  qui enim formavit omnia, ipse est,  et Israël virga hæreditatis ejus :  Dominus exercituum nomen illi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־כְאֵ֜לֶּה חֵ֣לֶק יַעֲקֹ֗ב כִּֽי־יוֹצֵ֤ר הַכֹּל֙ ה֔וּא וְיִ֨שְׂרָאֵ֔ל שֵׁ֖בֶט נַחֲלָת֑וֹ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The portion of Jacob is not like them: for he is the former of all things; and Israel is the rod of his inheritance: The Lord of hosts is his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congrega de terra confusionem tuam,  quæ habitas in obsidione :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִסְפִּ֥י מֵאֶ֖רֶץ כִּנְעָתֵ֑ךְ (ישבתי) [יֹשֶׁ֖בֶת] בַּמָּצֽוֹר׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Gather up thy wares out of the land, O inhabitant of the fortress.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego longe projiciam habitatores terræ in hac vice,  et tribulabo eos ita ut inveniantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־כֹה֙ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנְנִ֥י קוֹלֵ֛עַ אֶת־יוֹשְׁבֵ֥י הָאָ֖רֶץ בַּפַּ֣עַם הַזֹּ֑את וַהֲצֵרֹ֥תִי לָהֶ֖ם לְמַ֥עַן יִמְצָֽאוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: For thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will sling out the inhabitants of the land at this once, and will distress them, that they may find it so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ mihi super contritione mea :  pessima plaga mea.  Ego autem dixi :  Plane hæc infirmitas mea est, et portabo illam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֥וֹי לִי֙ עַל־שִׁבְרִ֔י נַחְלָ֖ה מַכָּתִ֑י וַאֲנִ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי אַ֛ךְ זֶ֥ה חֳלִ֖י וְאֶשָּׂאֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Woe is me for my hurt! my wound is grievous: but I said, Truly this is a grief, and I must bear it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabernaculum meum vastatum est ;  omnes funiculi mei dirupti sunt :  filii mei exierunt a me, et non subsistunt.  Non est qui extendat ultra tentorium meum,  et erigat pelles meas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇהֳלִ֣י שֻׁדָּ֔ד וְכׇל־מֵיתָרַ֖י נִתָּ֑קוּ בָּנַ֤י יְצָאֻ֙נִי֙ וְאֵינָ֔ם אֵין־נֹטֶ֥ה עוֹד֙ אׇֽהֳלִ֔י וּמֵקִ֖ים יְרִיעוֹתָֽי׃
verses.king_james: My tabernacle is spoiled, and all my cords are broken: my children are gone forth of me, and they are not: there is none to stretch forth my tent any more, and to set up my curtains.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia stulte egerunt pastores,  et Dominum non quæsierunt :  propterea non intellexerunt,  et omnis grex eorum dispersus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י נִבְעֲרוּ֙ הָרֹעִ֔ים וְאֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה לֹ֣א דָרָ֑שׁוּ עַל־כֵּן֙ לֹ֣א הִשְׂכִּ֔ילוּ וְכׇל־מַרְעִיתָ֖ם נָפֽוֹצָה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For the pastors are become brutish, and have not sought the Lord: therefore they shall not prosper, and all their flocks shall be scattered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox auditionis ecce venit,  et commotio magna de terra aquilonis :  ut ponat civitates Juda solitudinem,  et habitaculum draconum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֤וֹל שְׁמוּעָה֙ הִנֵּ֣ה בָאָ֔ה וְרַ֥עַשׁ גָּד֖וֹל מֵאֶ֣רֶץ צָפ֑וֹן לָשׂ֞וּם אֶת־עָרֵ֧י יְהוּדָ֛ה שְׁמָמָ֖ה מְע֥וֹן תַּנִּֽים׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Behold, the noise of the bruit is come, and a great commotion out of the north country, to make the cities of Judah desolate, and a den of dragons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio, Domine,  quia non est hominis via ejus,  nec viri est ut ambulet,  et dirigat gressus suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָדַ֣עְתִּי יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֛י לֹ֥א לָאָדָ֖ם דַּרְכּ֑וֹ לֹֽא־לְאִ֣ישׁ הֹלֵ֔ךְ וְהָכִ֖ין אֶֽת־צַעֲדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corripe me, Domine, verumtamen in judicio,  et non in furore tuo, ne forte ad nihilum redigas me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַסְּרֵ֥נִי יְהֹוָ֖ה אַךְ־בְּמִשְׁפָּ֑ט אַל־בְּאַפְּךָ֖ פֶּן־תַּמְעִטֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger, lest thou bring me to nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Effunde indignationem tuam super gentes quæ non cognoverunt te,  et super provincias quæ nomen tuum non invocaverunt :  quia comederunt Jacob, et devoraverunt eum,  et consumpserunt illum, et decus ejus dissipaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁפֹ֣ךְ חֲמָתְךָ֗ עַל־הַגּוֹיִם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹֽא־יְדָע֔וּךָ וְעַל֙ מִשְׁפָּח֔וֹת אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּשִׁמְךָ֖ לֹ֣א קָרָ֑אוּ כִּֽי־אָכְל֣וּ אֶֽת־יַעֲקֹ֗ב וַאֲכָלֻ֙הוּ֙ וַיְכַלֻּ֔הוּ וְאֶת־נָוֵ֖הוּ הֵשַֽׁמּוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Pour out thy fury upon the heathen that know thee not, and upon the families that call not on thy name: for they have eaten up Jacob, and devoured him, and consumed him, and have made his habitation desolate.
